message:
Add robot pure function tests (45.5% → 46.3%)

Test 11 untested pure functions: parseJFPIDs, normalizedProgramType,
escapeQuotes, formatTarget, summarizeReservations, detectReservationConflicts,
mergeBudgetConfig, buildEnsembleSuggestHints, buildEnsembleHints,
convertPTState, updatePTSummary. All at 92-100% coverage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2cs0","title":"Task: Query BV for in-progress beads","description":"## Purpose
Get currently in-progress beads from BV to show agents their assigned work.

## Scope
- Execute: bv --robot-triage or br list --status=in_progress --json
- Extract beads assigned to this session/agent
- Include bead titles, IDs, and blocking relationships
- Handle BV unavailable gracefully

## Bead Data Included
- ID: For reference (bd-42)
- Title: Task description
- Status: in_progress
- Blockers: Any blocking dependencies
- Priority: For ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- In-progress beads shown in recovery context
- Bead IDs formatted for agent reference
- Blockers listed to prevent wasted work
- Graceful degradation when BV unavailable

## Technical Notes
Use bv --robot-* commands for JSON output
Consider br list as fallback","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:34:49.803701317Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:55:28.020923732Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:55:28.020853790Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete - loadRecoveryBeads() in spawn.go uses bv.GetInProgressList","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cs0","depends_on_id":"bd-38kx","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2csfi","title":"Task: Decide TOON encoding strategy (Go lib vs CLI wrapper)","description":"## Overview
Choose the safest, lowest-dependency path to emit TOON.

## Options
1. **Go library**: embed TOON encoding directly in Go (preferred if stable library exists).
2. **CLI wrapper**: call the `tru` binary (toon_rust) if installed; Node `toon` is not allowed.
3. **Minimal encoder**: implement TOON subset for NTM payloads (uniform arrays + primitives).

## Inputs
- TOON reference implementation is JS (`toon-format/toon`).
- Spec lives in `toon-format/spec` (v3.0).

## Decision Criteria
- Reliability (no partial/invalid TOON)
- Minimal dependencies for Go build
- Graceful fallback behavior
- Token savings on real NTM payloads (see bd-rmnfk)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Documented decision and chosen path
- Follow-up tasks updated to match choice","notes":"Decision (2026-01-20): Use a minimal in-process TOON encoder in Go (no external CLI). Rationale: highest reliability + lowest deps; avoids Node/CLI availability failures; can strictly validate supported shapes. Scope: uniform arrays of objects + primitives; deterministic field order; tab delimiter only when safe; for unsupported shapes use JSON fallback in auto mode or error in explicit TOON.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T19:14:18.428574768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T21:01:06.221150114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T01:46:43.694079316Z","close_reason":"Decision documented: Go minimal in-process TOON encoder; no CLI dependency.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2csfi","depends_on_id":"bd-1fmug","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":65,"issue_id":"bd-2csfi","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Clarification: Node @toon-format/cli is not allowed; TOON must use tru (toon_rust) or in-process encoder.","created_at":"2026-01-24T20:57:47Z"},{"id":66,"issue_id":"bd-2csfi","author":"QuietCanyon","text":"Updated description to remove Node CLI reference; tru-only noted.","created_at":"2026-01-24T21:01:06Z"}]}

Implement a tools adapter for Destructive Command Guard (dcg).

## Responsibilities

- Health check returns clear message when dcg missing
- Guard active state can be surfaced in tool info when available","notes":"## Testing Requirements

- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-2d366","title":"Foundation: Define SwarmConfig struct and TOML schema","description":"## Purpose

Define the core configuration structure for the weighted multi-project swarm system. This struct will be embedded in the main NTM config and loaded from `config.toml`.

## Implementation

Create `internal/config/swarm.go` with:

```go
// SwarmConfig configures the weighted multi-project agent swarm system.
type SwarmConfig struct {
    // DefaultScanDir is the base directory to scan for projects (e.g., "/dp")
    DefaultScanDir string `toml:"default_scan_dir"`
    
    // Tier thresholds for bead-based allocation
    Tier1Threshold int `toml:"tier1_threshold"` // Default: 400
    Tier2Threshold int `toml:"tier2_threshold"` // Default: 100
    
    // Allocation per tier: {cc, cod, gmi}
    Tier1Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier1_allocation"`
    Tier2Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier2_allocation"`
    Tier3Allocation AllocationSpec `toml:"tier3_allocation"`
    
    // Session structure
    SessionsPerType int `toml:"sessions_per_type"` // Default: 3
    
    // Timing
    StaggerDelayMs int `toml:"stagger_delay_ms"` // Default: 300
    
    // Account rotation
    AutoRotateAccounts bool `toml:"auto_rotate_accounts"`
    
    // Limit detection patterns per agent type
    LimitPatterns map[string][]string `toml:"limit_patterns"`
    
    // Marching orders templates
    MarchingOrders MarchingOrdersConfig `toml:"marching_orders"`
}

type AllocationSpec struct {
    CC  int `toml:"cc"`
    Cod int `toml:"cod"`
    Gmi int `toml:"gmi"`
}

type MarchingOrdersConfig struct {
    Default string `toml:"default"`
    Review  string `toml:"review"`
}

// Defaults returns SwarmConfig with sensible defaults
func (c *SwarmConfig) Defaults() SwarmConfig {
    return SwarmConfig{
        DefaultScanDir:  "/dp",
        Tier1Threshold:  400,
        Tier2Threshold:  100,
        Tier1Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 4, Cod: 4, Gmi: 2},
        Tier2Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 3, Cod: 3, Gmi: 2},
        Tier3Allocation: AllocationSpec{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        SessionsPerType: 3,
        StaggerDelayMs:  300,
        LimitPatterns: map[string][]string{
            "cc":  {"You've hit your limit", "usage limit"},
            "cod": {"You've hit your usage limit"},
            "gmi": {"quota exceeded"},
        },
    }
}
```

## Integration

1. Add `Swarm SwarmConfig` field to main Config struct in `internal/config/config.go`
2. Ensure TOML unmarshaling works correctly
3. Add validation for thresholds (tier1 > tier2 > 0)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SwarmConfig struct defined with all fields
- [ ] Defaults() method returns sensible defaults
- [ ] Integrated into main Config struct
- [ ] TOML schema documented in comments
- [ ] Unit tests for defaults and validation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:57:53.819005935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T07:36:07.378565888Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T07:36:07.378520292Z","close_reason":"Implemented SwarmConfig struct with tier-based allocation, defaults, validation, and TOML support. Created swarm.go and swarm_test.go. All tests pass. Integrated into main Config struct.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2d366","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38jwx","title":"AutoRespawner: Force kill via process PID when needed","description":"## AutoRespawner: Force Kill via Process PID

### Purpose
Implement force kill fallback when graceful termination fails.

### Implementation Details

**File:** Part of `internal/swarm/auto_respawner.go`

**Force Kill:**
```go
// forceKill sends SIGKILL to the process in the pane.
func (r *AutoRespawner) forceKill(sessionPane string) error {
    log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] force_kill_start", "session_pane", sessionPane)
    
    // Get the PID of the foreground process in the pane
    // tmux display-message -p -t <pane> '#{pane_pid}'
    pid, err := r.getPanePID(sessionPane)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("get pane pid: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Kill the process tree
    // Use kill -9 on the process group for thorough cleanup
    cmd := exec.Command("kill", "-9", fmt.Sprintf("-%d", pid))
    if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
        // Try just the process if group kill fails
        cmd = exec.Command("kill", "-9", fmt.Sprintf("%d", pid))
        if err := cmd.Run(); err != nil {
            return fmt.Errorf("kill -9 failed: %w", err)
        }
    }
    
    log.Info("[AutoRespawner] force_kill_complete", "session_pane", sessionPane, "pid", pid)
    return nil
}

// getPanePID gets the foreground process PID from tmux.
func (r *AutoRespawner) getPanePID(sessionPane string) (int, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("/usr/bin/tmux", "display-message", "-p", "-t", sessionPane, "#{pane_pid}")
    output, err := cmd.Output()
    if err != nil {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("display-message: %w", err)
    }
    
    pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(output)))
    if err != nil {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("parse pid %q: %w", output, err)
    }
    
    return pid, nil
}
```

**Full Kill Sequence:**
```go
// killWithFallback tries graceful kill, then force kill.
func (r *AutoRespawner) killWithFallback(ctx context.Context, sessionPane, agentType string) error {
    // Try graceful kill first
    if err := r.killAgent(ctx, sessionPane, agentType); err != nil {
        log.Warn("[AutoRespawner] graceful_kill_failed", "error", err)
    }
    
    // Wait for exit
    if r.waitForExit(ctx, sessionPane, r.GracefulTimeout) {
        return nil
    }
    
    // Fall back to force kill
    if err := r.forceKill(sessionPane); err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("force kill failed: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Wait again briefly
    time.Sleep(500 * time.Millisecond)
    return nil
}
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log force kill start: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_start session_pane=%s`
- Log PID lookup: `[AutoRespawner] pid_lookup session_pane=%s pid=%d`
- Log force kill complete: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_complete session_pane=%s pid=%d`
- Log force kill failure: `[AutoRespawner] force_kill_failed session_pane=%s error=%v`

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Get PID via tmux display-message
- [ ] Kill process group with SIGKILL
- [ ] Fallback to individual process kill
- [ ] Combine with graceful kill in sequence","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:22.852466021Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:35.728016771Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T06:26:35.727966607Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38jwx","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-38jwx","depends_on_id":"bd-8uiw0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-38kx","title":"EPIC: Smart Session Recovery with Full Context Continuity","description":"**IDEA #1 - HIGHEST IMPACT (95% confidence)**

## What It Does
When NTM spawns agents—whether after a crash, reboot, manual restart, or returning after hours/days—it automatically reconstructs the context each agent needs to continue exactly where it left off.

## Why This Is The Best Idea
- The #1 frustration in multi-agent workflows is context loss
- Every session restart currently feels like starting over
- This transforms restarts from painful to seamless
- Users will feel like their agents have 'memory'

## How It Works
On session spawn/respawn:
1. Check for prior state: Look for existing checkpoints, Agent Mail threads, and session history
2. Query Agent Mail: Fetch the last N messages from threads this session participated in
3. Query CM (CASS Memory): Retrieve relevant procedural memories for this project
4. Query BV: Get current in-progress beads assigned to this session
5. Synthesize recovery prompt: Construct a structured 'here's what you were doing' context injection
6. Auto-send on spawn: Each agent receives this context automatically before user's first prompt

## Flywheel Alignment: Perfect
- Integrates checkpoints (already built)
- Integrates Agent Mail (already built)
- Integrates CM (part of flywheel)
- Integrates BV (part of flywheel)
- Each use makes recovery context richer

## Technical Implementation
- Changes to spawn.go: buildRecoveryContext() function
- New config options under [recovery] section
- Graceful degradation: works with any subset of integrations available

## Dependencies
Depends on: ntm init (for proper project setup)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:21:01.027989391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:49.544438840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:43:49.544326338Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-38kx","depends_on_id":"bd-38gl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-38x1","title":"Add ntm errors - show only error output from agents (filter for failures/exceptions)","description":"Add ntm errors command - show only error output from agents (filter for failures/exceptions).

## Problem Being Solved

When running multi-agent swarms, finding errors in the output is tedious. This command filters to show only error-related output.

## Command Design

```bash
ntm errors SESSION [--since DURATION] [--panes PANE_LIST] [--follow]
```

### Behavior
1. Captures output from all agent panes in SESSION
2. Filters for lines containing error patterns
3. Displays with pane identifier and timestamp

### Error Patterns
- Exception tracebacks (Python, JS, Go)
- 'error:', 'Error:', 'ERROR'
- 'failed:', 'Failed:', 'FAILED'
- 'panic:', stack traces
- Exit codes != 0

### Options
- --since: Only errors from last N duration (e.g., 5m)
- --panes: Comma-separated pane list to filter
- --follow: Stream new errors in real-time

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-errors=SESSION [--since=DURATION] [--panes=PANE_LIST]

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "session": "my_session",
  "errors": [
    {"pane": 2, "timestamp": "...", "line": "Error: file not found", "context": [...]}
  ],
  "total_errors": 5
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. ntm errors command exists and shows in help
2. Filters output to error patterns only
3. --since flag limits to recent errors
4. --panes flag filters to specific panes
5. --follow streams new errors in real-time
6. Robot mode returns JSON with error array
7. Context lines included (2 before, 2 after)

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/cmd/errors_test.go
- Test error pattern matching
- Test time filtering

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-ERRORS] ntm errors Command Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-ERRORS]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing ntm errors command"
# Note: requires session with actual errors to test
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. ntm errors command shows only error output
2. Filters for failures and exceptions
3. Supports session and pane filters
4. JSON output in robot mode
5. Color highlighting for errors
6. Unit tests for error detection
7. E2E test verifying error filtering","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-3954","title":"Task: Create FileReservationWatcher module","description":"## Purpose
Create a background watcher that monitors pane output and auto-reserves files.

## Module Location
internal/watcher/file_reservation.go

## FileReservationWatcher Struct
type FileReservationWatcher struct {
    client             *agentmail.Client
    projectDir         string
    activeReservations map[string]time.Time  // pane -> last activity
    mu                 sync.Mutex
}

## Key Methods
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) Start(ctx context.Context)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) checkPaneOutputs()
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) OnFileEdit(pane Pane, files []string)
func (w *FileReservationWatcher) releaseIdleReservations()

## Watcher Loop
- Poll every 10 seconds
- For each pane, capture recent output
- Parse for file edit patterns
- Reserve new files detected
- Release reservations for idle panes (10m+ inactive)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Background watcher starts with session
- Detects file edits from pane output
- Reserves files automatically in Agent Mail
- Releases reservations on idle
- Thread-safe operation

## Technical Notes
Use goroutine with context for lifecycle
Coordinate with session lifecycle events","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T05:41:19.522299399Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901182127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T16:11:37.901094762Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3954","depends_on_id":"bd-dz3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
